subject: First Aid & Safety
title: Innate Immunity: First Responders
description: One-minute skill: Innate Immunity: First Responders.

hook.text: Your skin blocks 99% of pathogens before they enter. It's your body's first and best wall.

buildup.text: Innate immunity includes physical barriers, chemical defenses, and rapid-response immune cells.

discovery.text: Neutrophils arrive within minutes, engulfing and destroying bacteria through a process called phagocytosis.

twist.text: Fever is innate immunity's weapon—most bacteria can't reproduce at temperatures above 100.4°F.

climax.text: Innate immunity is fast but not specific—it fights everything the same way without memory.

punchline.text: Skin, stomach acid, fever, killer cells. Your body fights before you notice.

quiz.question: Why does the body produce a fever during infection?
options: Most bacteria can't reproduce at elevated temperatures
To signal the brain that you're sick
